content:
Let   
 p(x)=ax³+bx²+cx+d,   
then   
 p(5)=125a+25b+5c+d,   
 p(25)=15625a+625b+25c+d.   
So  
 p(5)+p(25)=(125+15625)a+(25+625)b+(5+25)c+2d  
    =15750a+650b+30c+2d=1906.  
Divide by 2:  
 7875a+325b+15c+d=953,  
hence  
 d=953−(7875a+325b+15c).  

Now  
 p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d  
  =3375a+225b+15c+[953−7875a−325b−15c]  
  =953−4500a−100b.  

Set k=45a+b ∈ℤ.  Then  
 p(15)=953−100k,  
and we seek the minimum possible |953−100k| over k∈ℤ.  Since 953≡53 mod 100, the closest multiples of 100 are 900 and 1000, giving distances 53 and 47.  Hence  
 min |p(15)|=47.  

Answer: \boxed{47}.

Divide by 2:

hence  
 d=953−(7875a+325b+15c).  

Now  
 p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d  
  =3375a+225b+15c+[953−7875a−325b−15c]  
  =953−4500a−100b.

 p(15)=953−100k,  
and we seek the minimum possible |953−100k| over k∈ℤ.  Since 953≡53 mod 100, the closest multiples of 100 are 900 and 1000, giving distances 53 and 47.  Hence

Answer: \boxed{47}.
